title: What is NER?
concept: Named Entity Recognition identifies and classifies key information in text into predefined categories like names, organizations, and locations.

exercises.prompt: Why do we use Named Entity Recognition (NER) systems scan text? What is the main goal of Named Entity Recognition (NER)?}} Ends of words look the same to a computer. Why do we need Named Entity Recognition (NER)?

options.text: To find and label key details like names and locations
To translate text into different languages

exercises.explain: Computers read text as just a long string of letters. NER helps them spot and group important nouns so they can make sense of who and what is in the text.

cards.title: Finding Needles in Text Haystacks
cards.body: Think about how hard it would be to read thousands of news articles just to find mentions of your favorite video game company. What if an AI helper could scan them in a millisecond and pull out all the company names for you? That is the power of NER!

cards.title: What is NER?
cards.body: Named Entity Recognition (NER) is an AI trick that finds specific "real-world objects" in text—like names of people, companies, places, or dates—and labels them so computers can understand who or what the text is talking about.

cards.title: The Digital Highlighter
cards.body: Imagine you are reading a book with a yellow highlighter. Your job is to highlight every person in yellow, every place in blue, and every date in pink. NER is like having a super-fast digital highlighter that automatically colors and tags these key details for you!

cards.title: NER in Action
cards.body:
Look at this sentence: "Alice went to Paris in 2023." 

An NER tool reads this and labels:
• "Alice" as a **PERSON**
• "Paris" as a **LOCATION**
• "2023" as a **DATE**

cards.title: Myth: NER Understands the Whole Story
cards.body: Many people think NER understands the entire meaning and emotion of a story. It doesn't! NER is highly specialized: it only cares about spotting and labeling specific nouns like names, dates, and locations, rather than understanding the plot.

cards.title: Visualizing NER
cards.body: See how NER transforms plain, unstructured text into organized, labeled data categories.

exercises.prompt: What does the acronym NER stand for?
exercises.explain: NER stands for Named Entity Recognition. It recognizes "entities" (nouns like people, places, dates) and classifies them.

exercises.prompt: True or False: NER is mainly used to find action verbs like "run", "jump", and "swim" in a sentence.
exercises.explain: NER specifically looks for proper nouns and values like names, locations, dates, and organizations, not verbs or actions.

exercises.prompt: Fill in the missing word to describe how NER labels text.
exercises.sentence: NER identifies key words and groups them into predefined [blank] like people, places, and dates.
exercises.explain: NER labels entities by grouping them into predefined categories like names (PERSON), locations (GPE/LOC), or organizations (ORG).

exercises.prompt: An AI scans the sentence: "Sarah moved to London." Which AI output correctly identifies the entities?
exercises.explain: AI output A correctly identifies "London" as a location and "Sarah" as a person. Output B gets them swapped, which is incorrect.

exercises.prompt: You are building a tool to automatically extract shipping destinations and customer names from emails. Which task describes what NER will do here?

options.text: It corrects spelling errors and adds punctuation to messy sentences.
It identifies and categorizes key details like names, locations, and dates.
It translates text from one language to another automatically.
It writes creative stories based on a single prompt.

exercises.explain: NER stands for Named Entity Recognition. It extracts and labels specific entities like names (PERSON), locations (GPE/LOC), and dates from raw text, making it easier to analyze structured information.
